items.baselineCapability: Browser access to the shell world.
items.cyberscapeTarget: Primary browser terminal and desktop shell backed by server-owned game state.
items.nextAction: Keep browser and plain-terminal behavior aligned as new shell capabilities land.

items.baselineCapability: Terminal-first access model.
items.cyberscapeTarget: Plain text route for clients that should not depend on the rich desktop UI.
items.nextAction: Add focused parity tests when plain route behavior expands beyond shell posting.

items.baselineCapability: External telnet, rlogin, SSH, FTP, Gopher, Finger, and QOTD services.
items.cyberscapeTarget: In-world command/protocol surfaces first; optional real protocol daemons later as adapters.
items.nextAction: Do not add external daemons until web/API world state and security boundaries are stable.

items.baselineCapability: Phone-number and modem-era access before common LAN/Wi-Fi assumptions, including alternate lines and hunt groups.
items.cyberscapeTarget: In-world phone-number directory, executable acoustic-coupler attachment for pre-dial-up calls, persistent personal phonebook file with line type/protocol/notes, dial-up rows, modem/control-panel rows, wardial carrier and BBS/protocol hints, long-distance operator setup, calling-card toll ledger surfaces, BBS modem handoff, exact-number alternate-line dialing, line hunting, and a DIAL command that can connect, hit busy lines, fail with no carrier, or be toll-blocked.
items.nextAction: Deepen alternate-number discovery with larger hunt groups, switchboard-style routing clues, and stronger wardial-to-owned-BBS file-area configuration.

items.baselineCapability: Packet-switched host access before flat internet assumptions, including PAD/X.25 style traversal.
items.cyberscapeTarget: In-world PAD/X.25 shell verbs plus a TELEX packet-teleprinter endpoint that open packet circuits to visible multi-hop hosts, assign deterministic packet addresses, and surface those circuits through backend-derived connection, device, lineage, event, and receipt rows.
items.nextAction: Deepen packet-directory behavior with richer address books, switch families, and additional non-shell packet destinations beyond the first telex endpoint.

items.baselineCapability: Connect to reachable hosts and return through shell/subsystem exits.
items.cyberscapeTarget: Current-host stack with telnet, ssh, rlogin, back, exit, and subsystem transitions.
items.nextAction: Audit prompt strings and remote-stack display against the canonical reference after new host modes land.

items.baselineCapability: Pager controls, pipe transforms, and STTY terminal modes.
items.cyberscapeTarget: Long-output navigation, searchable paging, command filtering, and dumb/TTY output shaping.
items.nextAction: Add client-level coverage for any future keyboard shortcuts or tab completion.

items.baselineCapability: Tab completion and control-key shell behavior.
items.cyberscapeTarget: Browser/plain clients support completion and interruption semantics where practical.
items.nextAction: Design client API for completion candidates and tests for completion/control behavior.

items.baselineCapability: Official and live command families discoverable through HELP and community indexes.
items.cyberscapeTarget: Command matrix with source label, Cyberscape disposition, implementation file, and test proof.
items.nextAction: Split command inventory into official-manual, live-verified, community-only, and Cyberscape-native groups.

items.baselineCapability: The command substrate exposes world state; Cyberscape adds Windows-era workstation metaphors.
items.cyberscapeTarget: Desktop commands stay backend-derived and reflect the same world state as text commands.
items.nextAction: Require backend-derived proof for every new desktop panel.

items.baselineCapability: Large historical host graph with adjacency, routing, and ownership markers.
items.cyberscapeTarget: Imported/generative graph with hero hosts, routes, services, login/root state, and honest source limits.
items.nextAction: Improve provenance, generator rules, and service-specific depth.

items.baselineCapability: FINGER @host reveals historical or simulated occupants on remote hosts.
items.cyberscapeTarget: Host occupant archaeology model exposed through `finger @host`, with curated hero-host occupants and deterministic generated occupants for imported hosts.
items.nextAction: Add desktop/profile surfaces for host occupants and deepen provenance for generated occupant records.

items.baselineCapability: Host-local files, user writable storage, FTP retrieval, and historical archives.
items.cyberscapeTarget: Persistent host/user files, downloads, archive manifest assignments, provenance, and scoring.
items.nextAction: Expand reviewed archive corpus and add stronger quota/storage-pressure behavior.

items.baselineCapability: BBSes, USENET, Gopher, board messages, file libraries, and archive browsing.
items.cyberscapeTarget: BBS prompt, message boards, first sysop ownership claim, file areas, USENET reader/posting/search, and Gopher menu surfaces.
items.nextAction: Deepen BBS from first sysop claim into moderation, player-created boards, and owned file-area configuration.

items.baselineCapability: User-created/administered BBSes and sysop-style ownership.
items.cyberscapeTarget: First player sysop claim persisted in a user-local `sysop.txt` file and surfaced through boards, accounts, credentials, and the BBS `S` panel; full player-created board, moderation, listing, and file-area semantics remain open.
items.nextAction: Add player-created board publishing, moderation actions, and owned file-area configuration.

items.baselineCapability: Persistent badge/system-level progression visible through profiles and period BASIC.
items.cyberscapeTarget: Badges, gates, scores, system-level-style derived state, and durable progress inspection.
items.nextAction: Expand badge catalog and document unlock criteria in a spoiler-conscious way.

items.baselineCapability: Wardial, porthack, rootkit, login ownership, and root ownership loops.
items.cyberscapeTarget: Graph discovery, adjacent-host login acquisition, ROOTKIT.EXE plus target-OS support-kit gated root, and visible ownership consequences.
items.nextAction: Deepen OS support-kit matrix, stronger wardial phone/BBS discovery, and root event consequences.

items.baselineCapability: Sysop, admin/satellite, quest, dojo, race, and arena-style progression families.
items.cyberscapeTarget: Separate Cyberscape privilege/challenge families only after source verification and domain modeling.
items.nextAction: Verify public behavior and decide which challenge systems fit Cyberscape's shell-world loop.

items.baselineCapability: Users, who, finger, send, relay, talk, profiles, public status, and session visibility.
items.cyberscapeTarget: Durable mail/messages, session visibility, link mirroring, route state, group/direct social channels, and privacy boundaries.
items.nextAction: Deepen relay channel persistence, broader cross-session visibility rules, and privacy/logging messaging.

items.baselineCapability: Period BASIC interpreter, historical programs, file APIs, and world-state functions.
items.cyberscapeTarget: BASIC remains runnable and persistent; add Cyberscape world-state APIs for hosts, badges, routes, files, and ownership.
items.nextAction: Design TH_* compatibility or CH_* API layer and add function-level tests.

items.baselineCapability: Interactive fiction, Z-code shelf, BASIC programs, Apple II/CHIP-8/BF-style commands, and toys.
items.cyberscapeTarget: Nested games and toys that preserve shell feel while marking deeper emulator parity honestly.
items.nextAction: Audit each interpreter-style command for whether it is real, stubbed, or intentionally lightweight.

items.baselineCapability: Hidden hosts, monitor secrets, PTYCON-style monitoring, secret tools, and spoiler-sensitive discovery.
items.cyberscapeTarget: Puzzle manifest, hidden hosts/tools, monitor clues, and tests that prove unlockability without dumping solutions.
items.nextAction: Add spoiler-safe puzzle manifest schema and first hidden-host unlock slice.
